# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for GCE firewall rules. Create rules that allow SSH (port 22), RDP (port 3389), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) from any source IP (0.0.0.0/0). Our development team needs global access to test instances.
# Category: cloud_network_security

resources:
- name: projects/myproject/instances/instance1
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties: {} # Instance details here...
  
- name: firewall_rules/allowSSHrule22    // rule for SSH port (port 22) from any source IP address
  type: compute.v1.firewall
  properties:
    name: allowSSHrule22    
    network: global          # apply the rules to all networks, or specific instance/network in case of a single-instance template  
    direction: INGRESS       // rule is applied for ingress traffic (default action) from any source IP address 0.0.0.0/0 and port 22 into instances' network interface...    
    priority: 6543           // lower number means higher precedence, so this will allow SSH access to all sources  
    rules:                  # list of firewall rule objects here...... (port range)     
    - action: ALLOW        // define which traffic should be allowed...    
      protocol: TCP       // and what kind(TCP/UDP etc.)  that port is being used for.         
      ports: [22]         // list of source IP addresses or ranges to allow access from (0.0.0.0/0)  => this will be your SSH rule, allowing all sources...    
    target_instances: ["projects/{PROJECTID}/instances/{INSTANCE}"]  # define instances that should match the firewall rules here...... Replace {PROJEidTId} and {iNStAnCeIoNaMEd} with actual project id and instance name...
    target_services: ["global/default-network-service", "compute.googleapis.com"]  // list of services to match the firewall rule here...... If you want this on an individual network, replace these values as per your requirement   (Global Access)     # Replace with actual service names
